text:
Databases are essential for storing and organizing data. In the relational model, data is held in tables with rows and columns. To retrieve information, you use queries written in SQL, a standard language for managing structured data. For example, you can ask the database to show all customers from a specific city.

However, not all data fits neatly into tables. NoSQL databases offer flexibility for unstructured or rapidly changing data, using documents, graphs, or key-value pairs. Both types require careful data modeling—planning how information relates and is stored. To speed up searches, indexes are created, allowing the database to find records quickly without scanning every row. Understanding these concepts helps you choose the right database for your needs.
